title: $0.99 Subscription Micro-Charge as Card Validation Before Fraud Monetization
narrative: Fraudster initiates low-value subscription charges ($0.99-$2.99) to validate stolen card details before attempting high-value fraud. Pattern shows initial subscription charge to streaming/trial services (ProductCD='W' or 'C'), followed 12-72 hours later by rapid-fire transactions if card validates successfully. Initial charge uses temporary email domains (P_emaildomain in '10minutemail.com', 'guerrillamail.com') with mismatched billing/shipping addresses (dist1 > 500km). Card shows zero prior transaction history (card1_txn_count_1day = 0), then sudden burst of 8-15 transactions within 48 hours post-validation. Night-time initiation common (txn_hour between 1-5 AM cardholder timezone). AVS mismatch on initial probe (addr1 != issuer_addr1), but fraudster proceeds regardless. Chargeback filed 15-45 days later on initial subscription, after monetization complete.
indicators: Initial transaction $0.99-$2.99 to subscription MCC (5815, 5968) with card1_txn_count_1day = 0
P_emaildomain matches disposable email services list (10minutemail, guerrillamail, temp-mail)
dist1 > 500km indicating billing/shipping address mismatch on probe transaction
8-15 follow-on transactions within 48 hours if initial charge succeeds (card1_txn_count_2day spike)
Initial probe during txn_hour 1-5 AM in cardholder's timezone (is_night = 1)
AVS response 'N' or 'A' on initial charge but fraudster does not abandon
Chargeback filed on original subscription 15-45 days post-transaction with reason code 4863/10.4 (fraud)
summary: A brand-new card makes its first transaction as a $0.99-$2.99 subscription charge using a disposable email address and mismatched billing/shipping locations over 500km apart, then suddenly shows 8-15 additional transactions within 48 hours if the initial charge succeeds.
reasoning: Criminals test stolen cards with trivial charges that cardholders rarely notice immediately, then exploit the validated card for high-value fraud before the victim detects the initial probe and requests a chargeback weeks later.
indicator_explanations: Tiny first charge to streaming service from card with zero transaction history
Email is from known temporary/burner email provider often used for fraud testing
Billing and shipping locations are separated by impossible distance for legitimate subscriber
Card suddenly erupts with numerous purchases after the test charge clears successfully
First transaction happens in middle of night when cardholder is asleep
Address verification fails but attacker continues instead of abandoning the attempt
Original subscription generates fraud chargeback weeks later confirming stolen card usage
